name: Grant County State Bank
description_short: Locally owned community bank operating since 1913, offering full-service banking including checking, savings, CDs, and agricultural/commercial/consumer loans.
description_long:
Grant County State Bank is an independent, locally owned and operated community bank that has served families and businesses since 1913. Based on their website messaging, the bank emphasizes traditional relationship-based banking with what they describe as "old-fashioned customer service" and belief in "main street" values. The bank is managed locally and maintains a personal approach to banking, with their president John G. Schmid personally welcoming customers on their homepage.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of deposit accounts including regular checking, NOW checking, money market checking, savings accounts, and various certificate of deposit (CD) products (standard, IRA, and Roth). On the lending side, they provide agricultural loans (seasonal lines of credit, equipment, livestock, real estate), commercial loans (working capital, term loans, construction, inventory/equipment, SBA and USDA loans), and consumer loans (auto, debt consolidation, recreational vehicles, personal expenses). Additional services include internet banking, wire transfers, debit cards, direct deposit, safe deposit boxes, notary services, and bank drafts/cashier's checks.

What distinguishes Grant County State Bank is their explicit positioning as a locally owned alternative to larger banks, their long operational history since 1913, and their specialized lending expertise in agricultural financing (FSA and BIA certified lender status). They also maintain an anti-phishing policy clearly stated on their homepage to protect customers from fraud attempts.

Limitations include a basic, dated website design (noted as copyright 2009, last updated over a decade ago), limited online presence information, no mention of mobile banking apps or modern digital services beyond internet banking, and no published rates or terms available online. The bank appears to serve a rural/agricultural customer base primarily in the Grant County region rather than offering nationwide services. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Agricultural Loans (seasonal lines of credit, equipment, livestock, real estate)
Commercial Loans (working capital, term loans, construction, SBA, USDA)
Consumer Loans (auto, debt consolidation, recreational vehicles, personal expenses)
pros: FSA and BIA certified lender with specialized agricultural financing options including seasonal lines of credit and livestock loans
Locally owned and independently managed since 1913 with established community relationships
Comprehensive account types including Money Market Checking, multiple CD variants (standard, IRA, Roth)
Full-service lending across agricultural, commercial (SBA/USDA), and consumer segments
Explicit anti-phishing policy and commitment to not requesting account information via email
Offers wire transfers, safe deposit boxes, notary services, and bank drafts in-house
Direct deposit and internet banking available for account management
cons: Website appears severely outdated (copyright 2009) with minimal digital modernization, suggesting limited investment in online banking technology
No published interest rates, APR information, or fee schedules available on website
Limited information about service area—no clear indication of geographic coverage beyond Grant County
No mention of mobile app, mobile banking, or modern digital-first services
Appears to be a small regional bank with likely limited customer support hours and single location focus
best_for: Farm and agricultural business owners seeking FSA or BIA-certified agricultural lending
Small business owners in rural communities preferring relationship banking over large national banks
Local residents in Grant County seeking traditional community banking with personal service
diagnosis: Grant County State Bank is best for agricultural operators, small business owners, and rural community members seeking relationship-based banking with specialized agricultural lending expertise (FSA/BIA certification). Primary caveat: the outdated website and lack of published rates suggest this is a small regional bank requiring in-person contact for most services and rate inquiries, making it unsuitable for customers seeking modern digital banking or nationwide service.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
